title: fumaria macrosepala subsp. boissieri
intro: Fumaria macrosepala subsp. boissieri, a subspecies of the Fumaria macrosepala plant, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family Papaveraceae, commonly known as a type of fumitory. The plant contains alkaloids, including chelidonine and protopine, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Fumaria macrosepala subsp. boissieri include its potential to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Fumaria macrosepala subsp. boissieri can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
